evals.name: vertex-pro-ts-helper
evals.prompt: I'm adding an AI image feature to my Next.js app and we run everything on Vertex AI (GCP project 'acme-prod'). Write me a TypeScript helper using @google/genai that generates a 2K, 16:9 image with Nano Banana Pro from a text prompt and saves the resulting PNG to disk. Include the exact model id and the client setup for Vertex.
evals.expected_output: Uses model id gemini-3-pro-image (NOT a -preview suffix, NOT nano-banana-pro). Vertex client: new GoogleGenAI({ vertexai: true, project, location }). config.responseModalities includes IMAGE; resolution/ratio under config.imageConfig with imageSize: '2K' and aspectRatio: '16:9' (the shipped @google/genai SDK uses imageConfig, NOT responseFormat). Reads bytes from response.candidates[0].content.parts -> part.inlineData.data via Buffer.from(data,'base64'). Mentions ADC/service-account auth, not an API key.
expectations: Uses model id 'gemini-3-pro-image' (the current GA id), not a hallucinated name
Does NOT use 'nano-banana-pro' as a callable model string
Vertex client init: new GoogleGenAI({ vertexai: true, project, location })
Sets resolution/ratio under config.imageConfig (imageSize '2K', aspectRatio '16:9') — the shipped SDK field, NOT responseFormat
responseModalities includes IMAGE
Reads image bytes from response.candidates[0].content.parts -> part.inlineData.data via Buffer.from(data,'base64')
Uses GCP/ADC auth for Vertex (not a Gemini API key)

evals.name: flash-vs-pro-cost
evals.prompt: For my e-commerce app I need to generate around 50,000 product thumbnails a month plus a few hundred hero banners that have a lot of text on them. Which Nano Banana model should I use for each, roughly what will the images cost, and can I generate the thumbnails at 512px to save money?
evals.expected_output: Recommends Nano Banana 2 / gemini-3.1-flash-image for high-volume thumbnails and Nano Banana Pro / gemini-3-pro-image for the text-heavy banners. Knows 512 (0.5K) is Flash-only (Pro can't do 512). Cost: Flash 512 ~$0.045/image (so ~$2,250 for 50k), Pro 1K/2K ~$0.134/image; uses correct per-image numbers. May note text rendering is Pro's strength.
expectations: Recommends Nano Banana 2 / gemini-3.1-flash-image for the high-volume thumbnails
Recommends Nano Banana Pro / gemini-3-pro-image for the text-heavy hero banners
States 512 (0.5K) is Flash-only and Pro cannot generate 512
Uses correct per-image pricing: Flash 512 ~$0.045, Pro 1K/2K ~$0.134
Computes a sane monthly thumbnail cost (~$2,250 for 50k at $0.045)

evals.name: conversational-editor
evals.prompt: Help me build a conversational image editor: the user uploads a product photo, then types follow-up tweaks like 'make the background white' or 'add a soft shadow'. Using @google/genai against the Gemini API, how do I keep the image context across turns, and how many reference images can I send in one request? Show the code.
evals.expected_output: Uses ai.chats.create({ model, config }) + chat.sendMessage({ message }) for multi-turn context (the recommended way to iterate). Passes the uploaded image as an inlineData part (base64 mimeType+data) or Files API. States reference-image limits: up to 14 total; for Flash up to 10 objects + 4 characters (Pro 6 objects + 5 characters). config.responseModalities includes IMAGE. Correct model id (gemini-3.1-flash-image or gemini-3-pro-image, no -preview).
expectations: Uses ai.chats.create(...) + chat.sendMessage(...) to keep image context across turns
Passes the uploaded image as an inlineData part (base64 mimeType+data) or Files API
States reference-image limit: up to 14 total (Flash up to 10 objects + 4 characters)
Uses a correct GA model id (gemini-3.1-flash-image or gemini-3-pro-image), no -preview suffix
config.responseModalities includes IMAGE

evals.name: grounding-and-watermark
evals.prompt: Two questions for the Nano Banana image API: (1) can it generate an image that bakes in today's actual weather forecast for a city, and if so how do I enable that and which model do I need? (2) do all generated images get a watermark, and can I turn it off?
evals.expected_output: (1) Yes via Google Search grounding — add tools: [{ googleSearch: {} }] to config; Web Search grounding works on both Nano Banana 2 and Pro. (2) Every generated image carries an invisible SynthID watermark and there is no API flag to disable it. Distinguishes the consumer-app visible 'Gemini sparkle' watermark (tier policy) from the always-on invisible SynthID. Does not invent a disable parameter.
expectations: Enables grounding via tools: [{ googleSearch: {} }] in config
States Web Search grounding works on both Nano Banana 2 and Pro (not Pro-only)
States every generated image carries an invisible SynthID watermark
States there is NO API flag to disable the SynthID watermark
Does not invent a disable parameter; distinguishes consumer 'Gemini sparkle' from always-on SynthID
